entries.definition:
m–k–w to whistle, a certain bird with a long whistling sound;
buttocks; animal furrows, to wash, (of a horse) to sweat. Of this
root, only مُكَاء mukāʾ occurs once in the Qur’an.

مُكَاء mukāʾ [v. n.] whistling (8:35) وَمَا كَانَ صَلَاتُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكَاءً
وَتَصْدِيَةً their prayer at the House is nothing but a whistling and a
clapping of hands.
